name: hdfs_namenode
explanation: Despite the name, the HDFS NameNode check does not use JMX/RMI: it issues an HTTP GET against the Hadoop JMX servlet at `/jmx` on the NameNode HTTP port (default 9870 in Hadoop 3, 50070 in Hadoop 2). The Python check is a thin AgentCheck that joins the configured URI with `jmx` and parses the JSON beans (FSNamesystem, FSNamesystemState, NameNodeInfo). The path is canonical and singular, no auth is required by default, so a single HTTP probe against `http://%%host%%:9870/jmx` resolves discovery. Same pattern P as hdfs_datanode.
notes: Hadoop 2.x exposes the same servlet at port 50070; a discovery layer should probe both. Kerberos-secured clusters require SPNEGO credentials but the open default deployment is the assumed shape.
